questions.q: Who founded the Indian Statistical Institute?
questions.explanation: The Indian Statistical Institute was founded by the statistician Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis.

questions.q: In which year was the Indian Statistical Institute founded?
questions.explanation: The Indian Statistical Institute was established in 1931.

questions.q: In which city is the headquarters of the Indian Statistical Institute located?
questions.explanation: The institute's main campus and headquarters are in Kolkata (Calcutta).

questions.q: The Indian Statistical Institute is best known as a world centre for which field?
questions.explanation: The ISI is internationally renowned as a centre for statistics and, more recently, data science.

questions.q: The Indian Statistical Institute is commonly known by which abbreviation?
questions.explanation: The Indian Statistical Institute is widely abbreviated as ISI.

questions.q: P. C. Mahalanobis, the institute's founder, was primarily a specialist in which subject?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was a pioneering statistician, often called the father of modern statistics in India.

questions.q: The ISI is most closely associated with which broad area of study?
questions.explanation: The institute focuses on the development and application of statistics and data science.

questions.q: National Statistics Day in India is observed on the birthday of which person linked to the ISI?
questions.explanation: National Statistics Day is celebrated on 29 June, the birth anniversary of P. C. Mahalanobis.

questions.q: The Indian Statistical Institute primarily grants degrees and conducts research in which type of disciplines?
questions.explanation: The ISI specialises in statistics, mathematics, computer science and related quantitative fields.

questions.q: Which Indian statistician who built his reputation at the ISI has a famous formula named after him?
questions.explanation: C. R. Rao, long associated with the ISI, gave his name to results such as the Cramer-Rao bound and Rao-Blackwell theorem.

questions.q: Which statistical distance measure is named after the ISI's founder?
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis distance, a measure that accounts for correlations between variables, is named after P. C. Mahalanobis.

questions.q: In which year did the Indian Statistical Institute receive the status of an Institution of National Importance through an Act of Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Indian Statistical Institute Act of 1959 declared the ISI an institution of national importance.

questions.q: Which renowned scientific journal was founded by Mahalanobis at the ISI in 1933?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis founded Sankhya: The Indian Journal of Statistics in 1933.

questions.q: The ISI played a central role in designing which technique used in India's large-scale national surveys?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis and the ISI pioneered large-scale sample survey methodology, influencing surveys worldwide.

questions.q: Which national survey organisation in India grew out of Mahalanobis's pioneering survey work at the ISI?
questions.explanation: The National Sample Survey (NSS), launched in 1950, emerged from Mahalanobis's survey methodology at the ISI.

questions.q: Mahalanobis is credited with developing a two-sector economic model that shaped which of India's Five-Year Plans?
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis model formed the basis of India's Second Five-Year Plan, emphasising heavy industry.

questions.q: Which prestigious undergraduate degree, focused on statistics, is offered at the ISI?
questions.explanation: The ISI offers the well-regarded Bachelor of Statistics (B.Stat) degree along with the M.Stat.

questions.q: Where did Mahalanobis study, an experience that sparked his lifelong interest in statistics?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis studied at the University of Cambridge, where he encountered the journal Biometrika and the field of statistics.

questions.q: The ISI established a notable early research group in which computing-related field within Indian science?
questions.explanation: The ISI became a leading Indian centre for computer science, statistical computing and machine learning research.

questions.q: Apart from Kolkata, the ISI has major academic centres in which set of Indian cities?
questions.explanation: Besides Kolkata, the ISI operates important centres in Delhi and Bengaluru, among others.

questions.q: The ISI was originally founded within which department of Presidency College, Calcutta?
questions.explanation: The ISI grew out of the Statistical Laboratory that Mahalanobis set up in his rooms at Presidency College, Calcutta.

questions.q: Which Indian government ministry does the Indian Statistical Institute primarily operate under?
options: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
questions.explanation: The ISI functions largely under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.

questions.q: C. R. Rao, associated with the ISI, co-developed which theorem that improves an estimator using a sufficient statistic?
questions.explanation: The Rao-Blackwell theorem, co-named for C. R. Rao, shows how to improve an estimator by conditioning on a sufficient statistic.

questions.q: The ISI's full name in its founding era reflected work in 'pilot surveys.' What term did Mahalanobis coin for repeatedly resurveying part of a sample to control errors?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis introduced the technique of interpenetrating samples (subsamples) to assess and control survey errors.

questions.q: Which famous British statistician collaborated with Mahalanobis and visited the ISI, helping shape its early statistical research?
options: Both Ronald Fisher and Karl Pearson
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis corresponded and worked with both Karl Pearson and Ronald Fisher, who influenced the ISI's development.

questions.q: Mahalanobis applied statistics early in his career to a study of which physical phenomenon over Calcutta?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis made his early statistical contributions in anthropometry (e.g., Anglo-Indian measurements) and meteorology.

questions.q: The ISI is one of the few institutions in India that has long combined teaching with which two other core functions in a single mandate?
options: Research and the application of statistics to national problems
questions.explanation: The ISI's mandate uniquely unites teaching, research and the practical application of statistics to national problems.

questions.q: C. R. Rao's name also appears in which fundamental lower bound on the variance of an unbiased estimator?
questions.explanation: The Cramer-Rao bound, co-named for C. R. Rao of the ISI, gives a lower bound on the variance of any unbiased estimator.

questions.q: Mahalanobis served as honorary statistical adviser to which body, reflecting the ISI's national influence?
options: The Cabinet of the Government of India
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was appointed honorary statistical adviser to the Cabinet of the Government of India.

questions.q: Which mathematical geometry concept underlies the Mahalanobis distance that makes it differ from ordinary Euclidean distance?
options: The covariance (correlation) structure of the data
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis distance scales by the data's covariance matrix, accounting for correlations between variables unlike Euclidean distance.
